title: SQL Cheat Sheet
description: Searchable SQL reference — find syntax, examples, and usage for any SQL command instantly
viralHook: Stop Googling the same SQL syntax every time — bookmark this and never forget a JOIN again

fields.name: query
fields.label: Search commands, keywords, or concepts
fields.placeholder: e.g. JOIN, GROUP BY, subquery, HAVING...

rows.description: Retrieve columns from a table
Retrieve all columns from a table
Filter rows by a condition
Combine multiple filter conditions
Match against a list of values
Pattern match using wildcards (% = any, _ = one)

rows.syntax: WHERE col BETWEEN v1 AND v2
rows.description: Filter rows within a range (inclusive)
Sort results in ascending or descending order
Restrict number of rows returned
Skip m rows before returning results (pagination)
Return rows with matching keys in both tables

rows.syntax: LEFT JOIN t2 ON t1.id = t2.fk
rows.example: LEFT JOIN orders ON users.id = orders.user_id
rows.description: Return all left rows, NULLs for unmatched right rows

rows.syntax: RIGHT JOIN t2 ON t1.id = t2.fk
rows.example: RIGHT JOIN orders ON users.id = orders.user_id
rows.description: Return all right rows, NULLs for unmatched left rows

rows.syntax: FULL OUTER JOIN t2 ON t1.id = t2.fk
rows.example: FULL OUTER JOIN orders ON users.id = orders.user_id
rows.description: Return all rows from both tables

rows.example: SELECT dept, COUNT(*) FROM employees GROUP BY dept
rows.description: Group rows that share a value for aggregation
Filter groups after GROUP BY (like WHERE for aggregates)
Count rows or non-NULL values
Sum all numeric values in a column
Calculate the average of a numeric column
Return the highest or lowest value in a column
Add a new row to a table

rows.syntax: UPDATE t SET col=val WHERE condition
rows.example: UPDATE users SET active=false WHERE last_login < '2023-01-01'
rows.description: Modify existing rows matching a condition

rows.example: DELETE FROM sessions WHERE expired_at < NOW()
rows.description: Remove rows matching a condition

rows.example: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT)
rows.description: Create a new table with defined columns

rows.syntax: ALTER TABLE t ADD COLUMN col type
rows.example: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN bio TEXT
rows.description: Modify an existing table structure
Permanently delete a table and all its data
Create an index to speed up queries on a column

rows.example: EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM orders WHERE user_id = 5
rows.description: Show the query execution plan for optimization
